column4: visualizing proposed HRL habitat restoration projects
column5: State Water Board, members of the public, and other HRL participants
column6: Some proposed restoration projects have been identified and more will be identified in the future.
column8: The Sacramento Watershed boundary perimeter is a similar color as the stream network. I was also confused why only the Sacramento, Mokelumne, and Tuolumne watersheds are delineated. It might make more sense to develop watershed boundaries for each HRL tributary. It also took me a little while to locate the "about" button.
column12: The color of the Sacramento watershed boundary confused me at first, since it is a very similar color to the streams and parallel to the American River.
column19: If the acreage totals represent the project footprint, it should be clearly stated that they represent the project footprint, which may not necessarily equate to the outcome of habitat accounting. It is also unclear if "construction" refers to the date of completion of construction. It may be more useful to have a "status" label with clearly defined status steps, e.g., "design," "permitting," "construction," or "completed." For "target species" it would be helpful to further delineate the target life stages of each species, and/or Chinook salmon run if applicable. In addition, it would be helpful to include the specific HRL habitat commitment category that the project is intended to fulfill.
column25: In the filter tab, the exact acreage of each project is presented, while rounded acreage is presented in the detail tab when you click on a project. I think those should be switched, since the filter tab has acreage numbers very small and it's hard to differentiate a decimal from a comma. Thus, in the filter tab the MRDUA Large Floodplain Project initially appeared to be 58,427 acres, when it is actually 58.427 acres.
column28: "Fish Passage" and "Fish Screen installation or improvement."
column29: "other," since there did not appear to be any projects in that category.
column31: It appears that projects are allowed to have multiple assigned categories, but the filters only appear to select the first category. For example "Upstream of Old LaGrange Bridge Restoration Project" has 4 categories assigned but only appears if you select "fish food production" as the category.
column32: Should "rearing habitat" be "instream rearing habitat?" It appears to be mutually exclusive with "tributary floodplain habitat" so I believe that would be the correct renaming.
column34: How much Bypass habitat acreage is represented on the map
column36: The total count of acreage on the bottom.
column37: Unclear data accuracy. For example, the Tisdale Weir Reoperation project is quantified as 10,000 acres but the footprint on the map appears to be much smaller and it's unclear what the basis is for the 10,000 acre number.
column41: Unclear data quality (see prior comment about Tisdale Weir project), uncertain meaning of acreage numbers (see prior comment about acreage representing footprint vs. habitat accounting outcome), and incomplete representation of proposed habitat commitment.
column42: Fixes for the identified issues, commitment to updating map regularly.
column46: Contact info or location for more information about each project.
column47: Clear description of what the acreage number represents.

column19: Toward the top of the panel, the following text is shown "Delta Post-construction monitoring and science" but there is no link or info about "monitoring and science". The user would need to infer that this is a project phase detail.
column25: It was a bit difficult to discern which Feather River project I was viewing, although one of them was selected, it overlaps in proximity to the other and they aren't different colors, they are only differentiated by one become "bold" or showing a thicker outline, but in the same color, as opposed to popping up with a bright highlight to denote the selection.
column29: Watershed boundaries was unhelpful. . . because it is incomplete.

column37: While using the "layers" toggle panel to look at bypass projects. . . it wasn't apparent that on the other "projects" that only the "Feather" was selected from a previous search.  Maybe these could be expanded/combined? Or a "warning" could pop up for the user?
column40: Some of the descriptions seem legit.

column8: I see there is an "About" icon to click that gives the map's purpose but perhaps to make it more clear to the I would suggest inlcuding that basic descirption of the map's purpose up top on the title bar (or on a side bar; including it in the "Layers" bar might be a bit much).
column12: I found it very easy to navigate and find either by just zooming in, using the layers tab, or using the projects tab.
column19: Looked good to me. I think it would be helpful to have the current status of the project (e.g. in-planning, in-construction, completed, etc.). In the future I think it would be helpful to have pdfs to view/download (or links to pdfs) of the project design, CEQA documents, monitoring plans, annual reports, etc. or a link for a website with more information
column25: I was confused at the first that the Projects tab is dependent on what is clicked on the Layers tab. I can see why you did that. But I almost think it would be helpful if they were indpendent.

column43: Great work so far. Looks good! Thanks for your work on it.

column5: Could be the general public and/or stakeholders that are involved in some way with the HRL program or the Bay Delta Update. It is interesting that there is no mention of the Bay Delta Update here or acknowledgement that the HRL program will become the POI for the Update.

column8: The map formatting makes it hard to see the location of smaller projects until you zoom in. It could be cool to have the formatting change as the user zooms in and out, so at the coarsest scale perhaps the map displays a summary image organized by region or trib that shows the number of HRL projects in a particular part of the HRL project area.
column12: It is a simple and effective basemap with simple and basic information about the projects. I think there would be value in having a point of contact listed for each HRL project.
column13: It is humorous to me that we at CDFW all put effort into making this map as an initial effort to engage with HRL and that was 3 years ago and now we have what we were trying to make happen. Happy to see it get off the ground. There could be more specificity as to what HRL habitat acres each project is specifically seeking credit for.
